Initial_exploration_on_patient_location_data

Skylar Zeng

2025-07-21

Purpose

The purpose of this notebook is to tabulate and visualize patiend and location data of interest for the UM Sys Bio C diff data. They are:

  1. Descriptive statistice: patient data (age, gender, race, LOS)
  2. hospital vs. community onset of infection by MLST
  3. for inpatient infections, days between admission and infection onset by MLST
  4. Locations variation and temporal overlap

Demographics (n=727)

Age distribution (at least 18 yrs-old)

Interpretation: Strong skew toward older adults. This pattern is consistent with the known epidemiology of C. difficile, where older individuals — particularly those over 65 — are at increased risk due to more frequent healthcare exposure, antibiotic use, and comorbidities.

##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##   18.00   47.00   61.00   58.07   71.00  102.00

Gender

## 
##   F   M 
## 366 361

Race

Interpretation: The dataset is overwhelmingly composed of Caucasian patients, who represent 84.4% (610/723) of the total. African American patients make up the second-largest group at 10.9% (79). All other racial groups are represented in small numbers

## 
##                 African American American Indian or Alaska Native 
##                               79                                4 
##                            Asian                        Caucasian 
##                               10                              610 
##                            Other                          Unknown 
##                               19                                1

## Length of stay (days) Interpretation: - The peak in frequency occurs within the first 5 days, indicating that short stays are most common. - Median LOS is 8 days, with an interquartile range (IQR) from 5 to 18 days. - Several patients had very prolonged hospitalizations, including stays over 100 days, and a maximum LOS of 350 days.

##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##     0.0     5.0     8.0    14.1    18.0   350.0

Repeat above plot with secondary isolates highlighted

ST on HO vs. CO

For the rest of the plots/tables, I will only keep specific bins for ST types for which we have more than 50 isolates. The rest will get grouped in an “other” bin, along with the novel and non-determined sequence types according to Ariba.

Next I took the most common sequence types and broke down those counts by inpatient vs. not (again inpatient == 1)

And then hospital-onset vs. not

Next I looked at days until infection onset by sequence type for those isolates that were collected from inpatients (note does not restrict to hospital-onset only)

The raw data has outliers that make it hard to visualize. Removing those:

Still pretty scrunched at the bottom. Let’s look at a table instead:
